caption: Eight Elpidiidae holothurians, characterized by a gelatinous, elongated body, inhabit a soft sediment substrate alongside numerous slender-armed Ophiuroidea with a light coloration and rough texture.
A single specimen of Pannychia moseleyi, a member of the Holothuroidea class, exhibits a cylindrical shape with pale coloration and smooth texture, situated on fine sediment and rocky outcrops.

One Opisthoteuthis cf. californiana, a cephalopod belonging to the family Opisthoteuthidae, rests on a rocky substrate interspersed with fine sediment, displaying a rounded, soft-bodied morphology with a pinkish hue.
Six Pterasteridae, characterized by their star-shaped morphology and pale coloration, rest on a dark basaltic substrate near a black polypropylene drawer.
Three Solasteridae specimens, exhibiting characteristic star-shaped morphology, rest on a substrate of fine sediment and rocky debris, displaying soft, pale coloration.
At 1969 m depth, two specimens of Octocorallia (Family: nan) exhibit a branching, bushy morphology with a pale color and soft texture, anchored to a rocky substrate.
One Coryphaenoides, a deep-sea fish from the family Macrouridae, rests on a soft, sediment-covered substrate interspersed with organic debris.
A single, elongated, cylindrical body of Pannychia (Laetmogonidae) rests on the rugged, rocky substrate, its translucent, gelatinous texture evident.
One Bathyraja spinosissima, a member of the family Arhynchobatidae, rests on a rocky substrate, exhibiting a flattened, disc-like body with pale coloration and rough texture.
One Pteraster, a star-shaped member of the Pterasteridae family, rests on a substrate of angular basaltic rock, exhibiting pale coloration and a rough texture.
On rocky substrates at a depth of 1474.31 meters, a single Pannychia moseleyi exhibits a cylindrical, elongated shape with a rough texture.
Three Cystechinus giganteus, characterized by their rounded, pale bodies with radial spines, rest on a soft sedimentary substrate alongside three Ophiuroidea with slender arms.
One Antimora microlepis, a member of the Moridae family, rests on a rocky substrate interspersed with sediment, its elongated, streamlined body displaying dark, mottled coloration.
A single Bathyraja spinosissima, a member of the family Arhynchobatidae, rests on a soft sediment substrate, its flattened, disc-like body displaying mottled gray coloration.

At 1549.47m depth, a single Bathyraja spinosissima, characterized by a flattened, disc-shaped body with mottled gray coloration, rests on rocky substrate.

One Coryphaenoides, a deep-sea fish from the Macrouridae family, rests on a hard, rocky substrate with visible fissures and textures.

One Psychrolutes phrictus, a member of the Psychrolutidae family, exhibits a gelatinous, rounded body with pale coloration, resting on rocky sediment and overhanging structures.
A single, branching, fan-like colony of Paragorgia arborea, characterized by a pink hue and rough texture, is anchored on a hard basalt substrate.
A single, branching, bushy colony of Chrysogorgia pinnata, characterized by a pale coloration and textured surface, is anchored to a rocky substrate.
